pairs.question: What was the 1929–30 British Home Championship, and what significance did it hold in football history?
pairs.answer: The 1929–30 British Home Championship was an annual international football tournament involving the British Home Nations (England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland). It held significance as the 1930 FIFA World Cup began that same year, marking the tournament's first serious competition. The British nations, however, were not FIFA members due to disputes over professionalism in football abroad and declined invitations to the World Cup, viewing foreign teams as inferior competition.

pairs.question: Why did the British Home Nations choose not to participate in the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
pairs.answer: The British Home Nations were not members of FIFA at the time due to disagreements over the growing professionalism in continental and South American football. Even if invited, they indicated they would not attend, believing foreign opposition was too weak to provide a serious contest.

pairs.question: How did the England football team perform in the 1929–30 British Home Championship compared to previous years?
pairs.answer: The England team dominated the 1929–30 championship, winning all three matches and scoring fourteen goals. This contrasted with their performance the previous year, where they suffered their first defeat by a foreign team (Spain) and managed only draws against Germany and Austria.

pairs.question: What was notable about Scotland's performance in the 1929–30 British Home Championship?
pairs.answer: Scotland performed well in the tournament, winning against Wales and Northern Ireland before losing to England in the final match. However, their earlier struggles included being heavily defeated on tour in 1931 by Austria and Italy, and their first game outside the British Isles was not until 1929.

pairs.question: What was the scoring trend in the 1929–30 British Home Championship, and how did it reflect the playing style of the era?
pairs.answer: The tournament featured highly attacking play with very high scoring. The lowest total in any game was three goals, while two games saw seven goals, and two more had six goals. This reflected the popular attacking style of football in Britain at the time.

pairs.question: Which team won the 1929–30 British Home Championship, and what was their final record?
pairs.answer: England won the 1929–30 British Home Championship after winning all three of their matches, scoring a total of fourteen goals. Their dominance throughout the tournament secured them the title.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of the final match between England and Scotland in the 1929–30 British Home Championship?
pairs.answer: England defeated Scotland in the final match of the 1929–30 British Home Championship, securing their position as tournament winners. Scotland had previously won against Wales and Northern Ireland.
